title: Primary Key Management on Microsoft SQL Server
breadcrumbs: Primary Key Management on Microsoft SQL Server
text: Primary Key Management on Microsoft SQL Server Uniface uses the primary key specification to create a primary key with not null specified for the key fields. The key is created when the table is created, either on the fly or via the Create Table facility. Key names are constructed using the table name and the suffix _pk . The table name is truncated if it is too long to be contained within the MS-SQL identifier limit. All storage formats are allowed for key fields, except Bit, Text, and Image formats.
